meaning: v.咬,啃,啮,使苦恼,消耗,折磨,侵蚀
examples: I think eventually that question would gnaw at you and sour and override the pleasure.
It will also gnaw at Mr Summers, who leaves his job of repairing the economy unfinished.
The catch is that you have to be invested in order to gnaw on it.
No wonder: Such copycat drugs gnaw away billions of dollars in sales for the giants.
